coreIssue: This 1:1 is likely about whether Tom is translating Principal expectations on the new team into visible cross-team leverage rather than solid individual delivery.
openingQuestion: How are you deciding where to step in, ask for alignment early, and make tradeoffs visible as a Principal on this team?
listenFor: whether he names a recent project or stakeholder where he aligned expectations before work started
whether he describes Principal scope through a concrete decision, dependency, or cross-team tradeoff instead of only his own delivery
if they pause, stay abstract, or default to old-team comparisons when asked what Principal impact looks like here
avoid: do not turn the conversation into a scorecard of output when the question is scope, judgment, and influence
do not define Principal expectations for him too early before you hear how he is currently reading the role
goodOutcome: You and Tom have agreed what Principal-level impact on this team means in one concrete area of end-to-end ownership or cross-team decision authority.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent case where Tom delivered well but early alignment or cross-team judgment was still hard, so you can ground the conversation in specifics.
